reviews.text: Roger Senders is very knowledgeable, professional, discreet, accomodating, fair and affordable. I have used his legal and notary services multiple times. I do recommend him.

I had an amazing experience working with Roger Senders. He was the 3rd attorney I went to, trying to get help understanding the differences of my dad's original and revised Trust & Will.

He spoke to me with great patience, clarity, and knowledge. He was generous with his time and compassionate for what all this meant to me and my sister.  I can't recommend him enough and deeply trust his knowledge, professionalism, intelligence, fairness, care, honesty, patience, and willingness to offer more clarity if we needed.

Roger also consulted with a colleague regarding a specific CA law to ensure accuracy, which I really appreciated especially bc my dad had dementia when his Trust was changed with his wife. So I appreciate that he was very thorough and humble too.

His heart is pure & kind and his mind is very sharp. He is patient and his knowledge is extensive. Beyond valuable. Thank you deeply Roger for restoring peace in my heart, mind & family.

When I was in a crisis following a car accident and heard that the other party involved had hired an attorney, I began to panic. Rather than call up one of the numerous offices I had seen on TV in over the top advertisements I decided that it would be in my best interests to see someone local. I had driven past this office countless times while living in Culver City so when they popped up in a Google search I figured I would give them a shot. They were beyond helpful in listening to my concerns, helping me understand the scope of my situation, and giving me important advice and direction. I have not spoken to friendlier people in almost any industry, let alone the legal field, and will most certainly turn to them for any future needs I may have.

I have used Roger's services for years.  Living Trusts to Incorporating my business, drafting Service Agreements and more.  I couldn't be any happier with his professionalism, demeanor and his knowledge

reviews.text:
Five Stars! 6 if available! I am a member of the I.B.E.W. the international brotherhood of electrical workers.
I was injured in 2011. I was seriously hurt while working on the job. I thought that the "brotherhood" was going to take care of me. The IBEW has their own system for handling injured workers. Toward the end of their system, I was offered $75,000 to say thank you and goodbye. This injury was such that I could never again return to work, instant retirement. While going through the alternative dispute resolution process, I was informed that I should   seek counsel with Lawrence Stern esq. Since our first meet the wheels began to roll and in the early part of 2021 approximately 9 1/2 years later I ended up with a lifetime retirement, a lump sum of cash, which was many times more than the original $75,000 and a friend.
The one thing that I can say about Larry, is that if he believes in your case, you have a winner behind you. He reminds me of a racehorse, give him his head and get out of the way and hang on. It'll be one heck of a ride but worth it. He knows this business and he knows his job!! Honest and fair!
Joseph R. Evans

I seriously don't even know where to begin with this. When I first began looking for a Workers Comp (WC) attorney, Mallery & Stern were referred to me by individuals I hold a very high opinion of. I worked as a Law Enforcement Officer (LEO).

During my initial meeting, I met with Mr. Lawrence (Larry) Stern. It was myself and my wife. I sat down with Larry and he asked me what all I was experiencing injury wise and how long I had been employed in my field. Larry showed a lot of understanding and compassion towards me and my wife. Larry holds his clients in high regard. Larry told me he could help me and I had work to do as well by completing all documents in a timely manner and as accurately as possible.

Larry worked swiftly and had me seen by doctors and had all MRI's and necessary medical work completed fast. You need to be patient through this process as WC is very slow and will attempt to deny everything even when they accept responsibility for your injuries. This is where Larry is invaluable to you. The man is like a bulldog and will fight for everything you should be entitled to because WC will do their best to not give it to you.

My particular case took some time but without Larry on my side and him fighting for me every step of the way WC would have not given me what I was truly entitled too, they don't have you in your best interest .

I can tell you this, If you want the best of the best you need to give Mallery & Stern a call. You won't regret it.

These guys are the best! Read on....
We hired Sacks Law to defend against a bogus workers' comp claim. Their "30+ years of expertise" seems to be exclusively in the art of extracting maximum fees from their own clients' pockets with absolutely no interest of resolving the case at the most economical path.
​They used scare tactics, claiming the state would prosecute and we could lose everything, all while refusing to provide solid estimates. They would say you could lose over 200k in laundry list of liabilities from opposing counsel.  When I did the legwork myself and got the former "employee" to admit the claim was a "racket" (Alvandi Law recruits former employees willing to sign made up claims against former employers - another immoral firm) ,and agreed to drop all charges, Sacks Law still tried to insist on unnecessary hearings and legal fees—potentially another $20k–$30k to have the case dismissed on top of the 10k that they got for appearing to a request for hearing.
​I eventually took matters into my own hands: I helped the former "employee" fire their predatory counsel and file a dismissal directly with the court. (even after the case dismissal Alvandi Law had no clue they were fired haha!) Case closed for $0. If you don't have an insurance company with bottomless pockets for them to milk, RUN. Sacks Law have zero interest in finding an affordable or honest solution for small business owners.  They overcharged me for thousands and just got and email from them we have a balance of $290 LOL absolutely no shame or remorse.

Igor handled my situation with care and professionalism!  The service I received from him and the team was excellent!  Although I hope no one ever goes through what I have, rest assured that having him by your side is worth every penny!

I went to Legal Grind to find an attorney to prepare a living trust.  I was introduced to John Jennison.  That was in late June and I told him I would be out of town until July 10.  He said it would be ready by then.  It wasn't; it took until NOVEMBER.  When I went to pick it up, the price had gone up $50.  I took it home and looked at it couldn't believe the number of mistakes.  He had apparently taken another person's will and substituted my name for hers but almost everything about it pertained to her estate, not mine.  The beneficiary was unknown to me.  I went back to Legal Grind and requested an explanation but none was forthcoming.  I wrote to the California Bar Assn. but apparently that exists only to whitewash the incompetence of their members.  My money was refunded but the owner refused to speak to me.  Jennison's license has been suspended but that is because he didn't pay his dues.  AVOID THIS PLACE!!!

I am beyond grateful for the exceptional legal representation I received from Brent & Quinn Mayberry after the traumatic incident I experienced in 2017. Following a horrific accident caused by the reckless actions of a former friend, I was left not only with physical injuries but also a sense of emotional turmoil and uncertainty regarding my future. Mayberry Law stepped in at a time when I felt lost, and their expertise and dedication to my case were nothing short of life-changing.

From the moment I met them, they showed me nothing but compassion and empathy. They listened carefully to my story, made me feel heard, and reassured me that I wasn't alone in this fight for justice. Their professionalism, combined with a genuine desire to help me achieve the best possible outcome, stood out at every step of the way. They were always available to answer my questions and address my concerns, and they took the time to explain every legal detail clearly.

Thanks to their tireless efforts, Mayberry Law secured a favorable outcome in my case, helping me to hold the defendant accountable and seek damages for the pain and suffering I endured. Their unwavering support gave me the strength to move forward, and I’m forever grateful for everything they did for me.

If you are looking for attorneys who not only have the legal expertise but also truly care about their clients, I wholeheartedly recommend Brent & Quinn Mayberry. They didn’t just represent me—they fought for me, and for that, I will always be thankful.

reviews.text: I don’t usually write a review unless an experience was exceptionally good or exceptionally bad. Mayberry Law is one of those firms that gives a good name to law firms and lawyers everywhere. You get all the advantages of working with a brilliant seasoned attorney, partnered with his son, a gifted Harvard Law degreed millennial. Brett has spent his long career focusing on representing people who are hurt in personal injury cases. His demeanor alone gives you a sense of security that you’re in expert hands. He is thoughtful in his approach and clear in his explanations. He doesn’t just tell you what you want to hear. He tells it like it is, no sugar coating, no sales pitch. He’s honorable and decent in a field where it’s often not the case. His younger partner, Quinn, brings a fresh and unique perspective. Together, they are really a great team. I was referred to them right after my young son had been in a car accident. Both Brett and Quinn made themselves available at the first meeting and with very little notice. They spent time explaining the process and the possible outcomes, and I felt all the fear and pressure get lifted off me. I knew I could trust them. Thankfully, everything turned out alright for my son, and I am forever grateful to the Mayberry Law firm.

We were referred to Baldino Law, Inc. and his law practice, because the law office specializes in construction conflict work and knows construction business well. Before we engaged Ryan Baldino, we had spent a significant amount of money with the roofer, who did the original work, because we have a large multi-floor home and because we kept getting charged for services to correct problems that were caused by the roofing company. When we asked for the roofing company to make corrections, at their expense, they refused. Our nephew, who is also an attorney, referred us to Ryan because his expertise matched our needs. After Mr. Baldino got involved and started reprinting and advocating for us, initially the roofing company denied any liability, but after Attorney Ryan Baldino strongly advocated for us, the Roofing Company has agreed to make important remedial work, which we expect will correct the issues.  We highly recommend Ryan Baldino, Jim & Louise San Francisco

I needed a contract/construction attorney to advise and represent me in  a lawsuit against the painting contractor that destroyed my house.  Needless to say the entire job had to be repeated with new painters, carpenters, floor installers and screen installers.  I found Baldino Law right here in El Segundo, how fantastic is that?  I met with Ryan Baldino and was impresses immediately.  Usually, any first meeting is just to look at information and discuss fees.  This was completely different, Mr. Baldino had already looked up information about the painter and had a plan of action to discuss with me.  Ryan is very professional, low key; not pushy or rushed, he listened to what I had to say and gave me advice on how to  proceed on my own.  What?
Seriously, I could not believe my  ears.  Ryan told me if I had trouble proceeding on my own he would be glad to represent me.  How impressed would you be?  I know I was.

Of course we've all heard horror stories about attorney's.  But how often  do you hear or meet an attorney that assists you and advises you on how to proceed yourself?  I'm sure this isn't the case for all clients that Mr. Baldio meets with, my case was fairly small.  However, it's fantastic to know Mr. Baldino is available if I need him, for his fee of course.
Mr. Baldino is staying in my contact list for any one that needs a referral in his area of legal expertise.

It's nice to know Mr. Baldino lives and serves El Segundo.
Thank you again, Mr. Baldino.

I had a large contract for some remodeling, stucco, and an ADU. Contractor wrote up contract for the remodeling, stucco and ADU. But it went bad. I paid them $46k for the stucco and they wanted more money and not nearly done. There was no payment schedule. Side note: always get a payment schedule or don't sign contract. this is my biggest regret. End side note. Not only did he refuse to complete stucco, he fabricated invoices and got a mechanics lien recorded on my property. I never received any invoices. Then I got a foreclosure notice court date from his Lawyer. What a bully. It wasn't pretty and went on for 18 long months.
Then I retained Baldino Law services. Ryan is not a big lawyer firm and only charges a modest fee. I believe I hired Ryan's firm because he had review helping the common folk and was local (close to my house). But don't let the moderate fee or small firm fool you.  Ryan has the experience in handling big firm cases working in a big firm in his passed but has broken off to run his own practice.  Ryan is the top lawyer and has his own practice.  He felt he might as well work for himself because he's done the big cases for other lawyers in a big firm.  I feel you get your money's worth without the big firm price.  However, Ryan is truly an honest person who looks out for the common fold. He gave me a chance against these experienced bully contractors. Ryan was very good at listening. I go off the wall and Ryan kept bringing me back to look at the big picture and do one thing at a time. Ryan was looking out for our best interest from the start. He truly wanted us to get back into our home, then get this bully out of our lives. Ryan reminded them we had to go through mediation and arbitration per the contract. Ryan gave us the facts and this was going to cost us dollars ($$$). His best advice was to get our house in order which we did. Ryan came to my house and met with contractor and inspector. the contract and inspector were at each other’s throat. This would have been a $100k or more cost to me if this wasn't settled. The City inspector was willing work things out but the contract kept mouthing off and boasting all his work is to code when the inspector is trying to show him what he needs to inspect. Luckily i had Ryan there at my house. The inspector was walking off because of the contractor’s bad attitude, but Ryan was able to calm things down and got agreements to get the corrective actions done. bottom line, we got the permit final signed off. Next, was the liens and mediation. Ryan told me it would be in my best interest to settle with the contractor w/o going to mediation/arbitration. but when i this guy threatened to take my home from me and my wife, he and he did that to the last person who went up against him. Truly mean. Since that didn't work, we went to mediation. This process is very slow and took 6 months.
Even though we had a very good case, the best advice Ryan had was to settle and get them out of our lives for good. Which was the right advice. To fight, the win would cost 3 or 4 times the amount or more. Winning would be losing $$$. I guess if I were rich, I'd teach these guys a lesson. I wanted to. but my wife didn't want the negative and it would cause me high blood pressure, etc. Hold on to that negativity is very unhealthy and was causing me and my wife stress.
The best and only practical option was best to settle. Ryan got a zero-dollar settlement for us. not a win for either side. but Ryan got this terrible bully contractor out of our lives for good. A signed release for the mechanics lien and foreclosure case. What a relief and peace of mind this is finally over. Ryan, thank you so much for standing with us against such bullies. we truly appreciated your warm-hearted advice.
Truly a big thank you for our best outcome,
David and Leticia

As in-house counsel for a Contractor, we handle many of our legal disputes ourselves. However for more complex construction/real estate matters with difficult opposing parties, bad facts, or the potential for trial, Ryan has been our go to outside counsel. First, he's familiar with "the usual suspects" (opposing counsel, experts, mediators, judges) and has consistently been able to leverage those connections to cut through the BS and posturing and secure us fair deals. Two, he has impressive investigative skills and finds landmines/smoking guns buried in hundreds of pages of docs that others have missed; allowing us to set a perfect trap, or settle for cheap before the other side finds out. Third, because of his experience and the number of cases he has handled, his initial valuation/strategy of a case is nearly always right on the money and saved us tens of thousands in legal costs.

Ryan is easily one of my favorite attorneys because when we hire him I can rely on him to completely take the matter off my plate. I don't need to babysit him, pester him for follow ups, nor spend hours scrutinizing padded bills like you'd get with some other firms. He does good work and charges fair rates. He's the one I call whenever I have a difficult problem or nuanced question and he's never let me down.

Mr. Baldino was knowledgable, respectfully aggressive and assertive, and  appropriately persistent.  He was able to break down a complicated process and case in understandable terms and steps for our family.

What truly stood out was that he gave me his personal number and told me I could text him anytime if I needed help, even though I wasn’t his client and couldn’t afford his services. That level of generosity and integrity is rare.

I’m starting law school next year at Yale Law School, and he is genuinely a model of the kind of lawyer I hope to be. I represented myself in my case using the information Nigel provided me and I won yesterday. Thank you Nigel. You are not only a great attorney, but a great man.

reviews.text: To whom it may concern regarding the Nicolai Law Firm:                                               My name is Stacey, and I have a 95 year old mother. My mother was convinced by some people that she thought were friends of hers to hire an attorney to handle her estate several years ago. I was busy working and raising a family and I trusted these “friends” and the attorney. Bad mistake. Because of my mother’s age we recently decided to sell my mother’s house and have her move in with me and my husband. She has considerable assets and real estate. During the process of selling her home, we had to pull out the trust/will. We were absolutely shocked at what these supposed friends and attorney set up for her, myself, my children and grandchildren. Completely unacceptable, not to mention unethical. In a panic, we asked around to find someone that could “fix” the situation, and a dear, trusted friend suggested Adam Nicolai.           What a Godsend!                      During my first phone call with Adam, he put us at such ease. He told us that he would “take care of us” and boy, did he ever! He met with us immediately, rolled up his sleeves and dove in. His compassion and attention to detail blew us out of the water. He basically saved our financial lives. Did I mention that his family is part of his team? Wife Isa and son Bryan. They are fantastic as well.                         If you are ever in need of individual, family or business law, we are completely confident in recommending the Nicolai Law Firm to represent you. If you have any questions about our experience with Adam and his team, please feel free to contact me through Adam.                                                    So very impressed, and feel very safe with this man representing us.

reviews.text:
**January 2026 Update **

I still have not received receipts or proof of payment for medical payouts. Providing this documentation is well within the firm’s control.

** Update September 2025 **
I take no joy in this, and I recently reached out hoping we could resolve the matter amicably. That does not appear to be a mutual interest.

I identified more than $25,000 in procedures billed by providers that appear outside their licensed authority — including surgical and anesthetic codes. These were subtle billing codes not supported by their NPI registry, and the charges were taken directly from my disbursement. I do not know which attorney, counsel or co-counsel, was responsible for negotiating the liens, but I do know the funds were paid from a trust disbursed by Arias Sanguinetti. While it is understandable that complex coding can conceal overbilling, when I brought the discrepancy forward it was treated as though I were asking for favors.

His exact words were:

“After all we did to help you in this matter – and knowing your issues were mainly with Hess, you issued a very bad review of my firm. Which is the only negative review of my firm. Considering that review, we are not inclined to do anything at this point.”

Unfortunately, as direct responses have not been fruitful, I have taken the matter to the California Attorney General, where additional issues have also been raised.

>>>
This firm came into the case as co-counsel, brought in by another attorney who is now facing disciplinary proceedings with the State Bar. That’s a separate matter—but there are similarities in how both approached their duties to me as a client.

For over two years now, Arias Sanguinetti Wang & Team, LLP has refused to provide receipts or proof of payment for over $300,000 in medical liens that were deducted from my settlement. Despite repeated requests, they’ve withheld documentation that I am legally entitled to as a client.

They did show proof of payment to the State Bar—just enough to stall further investigation—but have refused to provide those same documents directly to me. That raises serious concerns about transparency and intent.

I went through non-binding arbitration, and the responses was a masterclass in creative writing—mixing in partial truths, omissions, and outright falsehoods. During the hearing, Mike Arias behaved like we were longtime friends, repeatedly interrupting and sidestepping accountability. At that December 2023 proceeding, Arias promised to send me proof of payment. To this day, he has not.

I have since taken the matter to the California Attorney General, where additional issues have also been raised.
reviews.ownerResponse: We’re happy we were able to resolve your case in a very favorable settlement and we stand by that result. We’re sorry that you are displeased with your prior counsel and the rates that he negotiated but, unfortunately, we cannot assist you in that matter. That is between you and your prior counsel.

reviews.text:
Former client of Matthew J. Kita. I was unable to locate the Texas office page to leave a review, so I am posting here for the Los Angeles office.

As a survivor of sexual assault involving a high-powered CEO, I want to express my sincere gratitude for Mr. Kita’s representation. He was a zealous advocate on my behalf at the appellate level, while consistently demonstrating exceptional character, integrity, and respect for the rule of law.

I recently obtained the court audio—now available as previously sealed materials are being released—and I was deeply moved listening to Mr. Kita argue my case. Hearing him in the appellate courtroom, advocating with such precision, strength, and clarity, brought me to tears. He fought relentlessly, yet with elegance and professionalism, grounding every argument firmly in the law.

In a time when more survivors may feel empowered to come forward, it matters profoundly to have counsel who truly understands both the legal and human dimensions of these cases. Mr. Kita advocates for women with a level of conviction, competence, and moral clarity that is rare. He is unafraid to go toe-to-toe with even the most formidable opposing counsel, and he does so with reason, credibility, and common sense.
I remain deeply grateful for his advocacy.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your generous review of Matthew and our firm – this was very compelling to read. Everything we do is in pursuit of giving clients an experience of justice like the one you describe here.

I have just been scammed on the internet for 6-Figures and it has been a tremendous road back to recovery to say the least. Anyone who has been scammed or hacked knows full well the feeling of vulnerability, anger, despair, rage and the stun that is left to permeate & linger everyday afterwards.
I have had e very informative, very considerate & very kindred on-line video call with Uri, an attorney with Arias Sanguinetti Wang & Team Law Firm and I have to say that I as oriented on the various aspects of scams in a way that helped me make more sense of it all and not the blurry choices of what one need & has to make after being attacked and plundered of you finances by ruthless & unconscionable scammers. This was the first open arms to listen and get feedback about the many aspects of being a victim to scammers...that it allowed me to fathom what happened better, to not beat myself up and realize that these are seasoned criminals who target the most potentially profitable victim they can find, I was able to appreciate these words of experience and knowledge about the various ways I could have addressed it if I had only known or sought expert advice before I allowed the, to control the process my account.
Uri pointed out many things I might have considered and many things one can do, so I didn't feel so alone in the aftermath of what I have needed to continue to do. Uri and this law firm, have been attentive, considerate and very detailed in what happened, what I can nor can't do depending on the case...so I have great respect & appreciation for the time Uri took to help me get past the big hurdles of this scam...and I can only hope, that more law firms will do this very important professional courtesy for their clientele. Thank you Uri & all your law firm for your genuine time & consideration to my issue. My best to you...

reviews.text: In late 2021 I suffered a workplace injury involving multiple body parts.  In the beginning I was dealing solely with workers comp for treatment and had obtained a workers comp attorney.  Unsatisfied with the level of treatment I was receiving for my injuries, I decided it might be best if I sought out the services of a personal injury attorney.  A close friend directed me to a relative who practiced outside of Los Angeles who in turn connected me with Jaime Goldstein, a trial lawyer with Arias Sanguinetti law firm.  From the beginning to the end, Jaime was very compassionate, professional, informative, and extremely helpful in helping me navigate this entire ordeal.  When workers comp was giving me the runaround she also found treatment options for me, and got me on the road to healing.  During the deposition she advocated for me.  When it came time for the mediation and settlement offer, she fought to get me the best possible outcome - which ended up being about 3 times the figure I thought I might receive.  She was also able to negotiate with workers comp on my behalf to have those medical liens completely dismissed.  All together she went above and beyond and I couldn’t be more thankful for her.  I highly recommend Jaime Goldstein, and Arias Sanguinetti law firm.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your story. We are proud to hear how Jamie made you feel supported and led with advocacy to successfully deliver justice in your case!

reviews.text: I tried dealing directly with my car manufacturer 3 times over several months and got nowhere. I was told my Lemon Law case was denied and closed and they would not reopen my case. So, I reached out to Josh (among a few other attorneys). Josh gave me a no-pressure, factual consultation. After I decided to hire him and signed on, he jumped into action immediately, and literally by the next morning I had a note from the manufacturer that they would buy back my car. They reached out to me directly, trying to get me to abandon using an attorney, but I'm glad I kept working with Josh. He made the process simple for me, guided me along the way, handled all communication, and even drove 2 counties down to attend the vehicle surrender with me. I had a couple of panics along the way, and he talked me down and got things done with zero problems. I highly recommend working with Josh if you have a lemon!

Josh was incredible and I highly recommend him. I contacted him for help with getting my Nissan leaf bought back. The process was super easy, all his fees were paid by Nissan, and I had a check in hand and my car bought back in a month without any trips to the dealership required. He was able to argue with Nissan to have them backdate the issue which earned me nearly twice as much as I expected. I was connected to Josh via ChatGPT, which made me a little nervous at first but I’m so glad I went ahead with him.

I had a great experience working with Joshua on my Jeep lemon law case. He was on top of everything from start to finish. Always responsive, answered all of my questions, and proactively reached out to the manufacturer on my behalf.
The entire process was smooth and stress-free, and my case was resolved in less than two months. I honestly can’t say enough good things about Joshua and his professionalism.
If you’re dealing with a lemon law issue, I highly recommend Joshua. You’ll be in good hands.

Last year on my way to work I was in a major motorcycle accident, snapped my left femur, shattered my right knee, broke my left wrist, and sprained my right wrist. I was almost like those cartoons where someone gets hurt and they wrap them up in a full body cast, I was completely broken and useless, both emotionally and physically. Unfortunately for me I had NO medical insurance at the time due to some complicated work issues and had no real savings. I can't describe the feeling of hopelessness I had. That's where the law offices of Gabriel H. Avina came in. Recommended to me by a friend, they drove far to meet me at the hospital and took care of EVERYTHING. I mean EVERYTHING. They dealt my motorcycle insurance, got my destroyed bike towed to my house from the impound, did all contact with the other party I was in the collision with (I literally had zero contact with them which was very helpful for my mental health), negotiated my medical bills, got an amazing settlement, and filled out every single piece of paper work you could think of, all while I was laying down with every limb broken. I literally didn't have to get out of bed, think, or stress about anything. They must have driven hours in traffic back and forth to the crash site, hospital, my house, rehab, etc. Above and beyond is an understatement. If you don't know a lawyer and are aimlessly googling, stop right here, you found them. This is what you were looking for. This review was a long time coming, I wanted to put everything behind me and not think about it anymore until I was ready. Thank you for everything

Throughout the the entire journey there were multiple aggressive negotiation (none of which I had to do any work at all) with not only the insurance company, but also with the medical companies. Every step of the way I was informed of all my choices and options and they always let me decide which way to take the case. Galina was the woman I dealt with mostly and she was amazing. She never allowed me to go more than a day without hearing back from her. Anything that I didn’t understand, she explained to me very clearly and thoroughly - which I appreciated as I’ve never “hired a lawyer” before. I didn’t have to pay anything out of pocket, as part of the settlement went to them which was really nice for me because I didn’t have to stress over money during the litigations. 10/10 would hire again, however my goal is to never talk to them again because if I do it means I was probably in another motorcycle accident.

I initially hired Rachel Silverman of VSB to represent me in my divorce 6 years ago and was impressed with her services as she helped me to obtain a settlement that I could be happy with. A few years later, I needed to hire her again to deal with my ex-husband's refusal to continue required payments, which was in violation of the original divorce agreement. Rachel helped me to come to a good resolution of the most recent case against my ex, and I loved what she was able to accomplish despite the fact of how difficult it is to work with my ex! Recently, my ex even went as far as to provide a review on Rachel and her firm despite the fact that he wasn't even a client which makes absolutely no sense! If you are considering using Rachel and VSB I would disregard my ex's review as it doesn't apply to how hard Rachel and VSB work for their clients.

I have nothing but wonderful things to say about this firm. I recently went through a divorce, and I felt so safe and cared for by Scott Braybrooke and Rachel Silverman. They were always so quick to respond, and handled my questions with such compassion and clarity. During an emotionally stressful time, I really felt like they cared about my specific situation and always advised me with my best interests in mind.

I recently settled a 3-year long divorce case against my ex-wife who has been represented by Rachel Silverman for the last many years. The settlement was extremely favorable for me, so this is written not as an embittered loser but as someone who witnessed what seemed to be bad representation in my opinion, one in which the lawyers ended up with almost all the money, leaving my ex-wife with pennies on the dollar.

Take my review it with a grain of salt if you wish; it is just my opinion and, given that the attorney used her relationships in the community to build trust in the South Bay, it just seems some caution should be warranted. I explain below.

The facts of the case were that my income significantly reduced 2 years after our initial divorce settlement almost 5 years ago. I indicated to my ex-wife that my income had reduced and I asked for some patience while I got things back on track. I continued to pay a high percentage of my monthly requirements, just couldn't pay 100% of it.

Sadly, my ex re-hired Rachel (who had represented her during the initial settlement) to come after me. While I was not the perfect defendant (slowly providing the requested documentation because I was just too busy to spend the days on the tens of thousands of documents requested unnecessarily), I provided all the materials needed to prove that my income had reduced.

Despite thousands and thousands of pages of tax returns, Quickbooks files, bank records, credit card records, pay stubs I was forced to hand over, they simply would not believe that my income had reduced, even writing that in their final briefs to the court explaining their unwillingness to settle.

During the months and years after which they had access to all the records they needed to know that my income had reduced, not once did Rachel ever acknowledge my new situation, instead continuing to file motions to compel even more documents and seek sanctions, at a high cost to my ex wife and our children.

It took me filing two RFOs against my ex to essentially bring the case to a head. Each RFO cost my ex perhaps tens of thousands of dollars, but Rachel still didn't advocate for a settlement. They had the tax returns, they had the Quickbooks files, they had the pay stubs, they had the bank records. Proof was easy to see.

Throughout the three years, I had repeatedly told both Rachel and my ex that there was no extra money and that I had no intention of ditching any of my obligations--I had never missed or objected to a single payment until my income reduction.

Yet almost three years later and $50,000 spent by my ex wife on Rachel, it took a mandatory settlement judge screaming at Rachel and my ex, pointing out the that my ex would never receive her lawyers fees and should have stopped receiving spousal support two years ago and would not receive nearly the amount Rachel was asking for to end the ridiculous case. Strangely, Rachel seemed to believe she would be awarded her fees, too. Because she wasn't, my ex sadly had to pay it. It hurts me to know that two years of college savings funds were spent on Rachel.

I can't ever know or even claim to know what was going through Rachel's (or my ex's heads) during the case, and it is quite possible that my ex was driving this despite objections by Rachel, but this case should have been stopped years ago when it became apparent that my income had actually reduced as I had claimed and proved.

So, while I respect Rachel's doggedness (she does know the law, and it was scary for me as I decided to represent myself when I didn't want to waste fees on lawyers), to see the judge literally scream at her and my ex was a sight to be seen. In the end, Rachel got almost 70% of the money my ex was awarded, which was only about 1/3 of what I would otherwise have had to pay. So, not a good result for my ex. It saddens me tremendously that so much money was wasted and that my ex ended up with 10 cents on the dollar.

Rachel does have skills, but it looked from the outside that she massively misread this case and thought she'd be awarded fees.

reviews.ownerResponse:
We have no record of this individual as a client of our firm. The content of this review including the suspect promotion of another law firm, strongly suggests it was not posted in good faith or based on any actual experience with us.

Our firm maintains detailed case management records, and we take client communication and continuity of representation seriously. If any current or former client ever has concerns, we address them directly and professionally.

The hundreds of five star reviews posted by real clients reflect the standard of service and professionalism we bring to every case. We remain committed to providing legitimate, results-driven legal representation to our clients throughout Southern California.

reviews.text:
I signed my contract with these office in April 2025. I ended my contract with these people in November 2025 for the following reasons for 7 months. I got zero treatment because they claimed there was no Orthopedics  doctors in the LA area that I could  treat me . I’ve never got my correct temporary disability pay due to me from Workmen’s Comp  as well because they kept telling me that my math was wrong only to admit at the very end that my math was correct and theirs was wrong. I was evicted from my apartment in May 2025 because there was never enough money from temporary disability for
Workman’s  comp. They suggested that I live in my car and be homeless as so many people does that in LA already. In September they recommended that I moved back to Los Angeles so I can get Surgery on my lag. And I did so. Only to find out that the doctor they sent me to it was only a consultant who could not actually do leg surgery so I wasted my time in the moved to Los Angeles for no reason at all. To this day, I’ve never received treatment for my disability as I was severely hurt at work. I have been told that I must get total hip replacement surgery or lose my leg by 15 different orthopedic surgeons. But none of those surgeons would do the surgery because they’re not allowed to for whatever reason according to the insurance. I never get my correct TD pay as well . I have literally sent 40-50 maybe even 100 emails about these issues and they’ve always been ignored, A personal friend of mine who  Referred  me, to this attorney who is a QME doctor For the state of California is a aware of everything that has been going on who referred me to this office and he has now told me that he will never refer anyone else to this office because of what they’ve done. I am aware that there’s been other clients who had the same problems and they’ve also had to leave and find legal assistance elsewhere. Today I received the email stating that they no longer want to work with me because I have unrealistic expectations for medical care. I guess the fact that if I don’t get the surgery, I can lose my entire leg is not that very  important. Well for 7 months I had no medical care. The best thing you could do is keep everything documented because they lose documents quite often they also have my son a case because he was injured in a car accident, and they have no clue about documents that I have personally sent to them because they keep losing them. All I can say is if you few sign up with this office, understand the risk understand that you better not expect no medical care because they told me that today in writing. You sign up at your own risk
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share your perspective. We genuinely understand how stressful and overwhelming a workers’ compensation injury can be, especially when medical treatment and disability payments are delayed by the insurance company. We never take those hardships lightly.

That said, your review does contain several statements that are not accurate, and we feel it’s important to clarify them for the sake of transparency.

We did not advise you to become homeless or live in your car. Our records show that our staff tried to work with you on logistics, transportation, and available treatment options, and we never told you to relocate or uproot your living situation.

Regarding communication, we responded to your emails and calls, and we do not ignore clients. We also maintain detailed records for every file, including yours and your son’s. We are sorry if there were misunderstandings or if our efforts were not aligned with your expectations.

Ultimately, we made the decision to start the withdrawal process because you were abusive towards our staff. We stand behind ALL of our reviews and believe it is in every client’s best interest to work with a firm they feel confident in and aligned with.

We truly wish you the best moving forward and hope you are able to obtain the treatment you need as soon as possible.

reviews.text: I had a five-star experience with this law-firm defending against an anti-SLAPP motion. James and Griffin are excellent attorneys with extensive knowledge across all aspects of the law. They are highly detail-oriented and have outstanding drafting skills. Just as importantly, they always listen to their clients, keep them informed, and act in their best interests. They reach out only when necessary, which shows genuine sensitivity to their clients’ time and budget.

James Decker did an outstanding job in getting my trial court dismissal reversed in the Court of Appeal.  James is a highly competent attorney of great integrity.  The briefs he filed on my behalf were exceptionally well-written.  He also did a very good job summarizing our position and fielding questions during the oral argument phase.  Without reservation, I highly recommend James Decker as an attorney.

Griffin and James provided extraordinary legal service for us! We faced a challenging case where the other side, in my opinion, made the process quite complicated.
Thankfully, we had Griffin and James on our side. They were honest, sympathetic, and professional. Their calm approach and expertise really shielded us from the stress, and they handled complex situations efficiently—often resolving issues behind the scenes without our involvement. Whenever our input was needed, they gave clear guidance and thoughtful advice so we could make the best decisions.
In the end, we prevailed completely and were even awarded our legal fees in full. We were so impressed with their work that we gave them a bonus to show our appreciation.
We couldn’t have asked for better lawyers — thank you, Griffin and James! It was an absolute pleasure working with you both.

If you are a nurse, in a health field, or just anyone in need of an exceptional Appeals Attorney, I urge you to read this review. Griffin Schindler of Decker Law will fight his hardest with legal expertise, strategic insight, and relentless advocacy.
What Griffin Schindler has done for me is nothing short of a miracle. Twice.  I am grateful to him for having my Nurse Practitioner license reinstated. He took on my cases and won when several other attorneys would not, warning me that they were nearly impossible to win.
After years of attorneys who promised me trial but did not after taking my money, I desperately succumbed to the belief that legally, you get what you pay for. I paid dearly for Vince Tucci, who promised me trial or a resolution equal to or better. After being pressured and misled by Vince Tucci - who had touted himself as Legal Jesus - into accepting the Guilty Plea, I lost my right to trial and soon thereafter the Board of Nursing revoked my nursing license. I immediately sought to overturn the Guilty Plea. Despite the inordinate difficulty of overturning a guilty plea, I held faith in the justice system and found Griffin. His knowledge of the law, courtroom skills, and conviction for me led to achieving the reversal of the Guilty Plea, with the judge stating that I had been “bullied” and “strong-armed” into accepting it.
I retained Griffin again to fight the California Board of Nursing to rescind the Revocation of my nursing licenses. He argued that the Board must in light of the fact that the convictions were vacated and void, but the Board’s Deputy Attorney General Craig Menchin refused.  I was repeatedly reminded by my nurse and doctor peers, friends, and other attorneys that appealing the Board’s decision was nearly impossible and would cost me more money and time due to The California Board of Nursing being untouchable behind the façade of protecting the public. At times, I grew doubtful and fearful, but Griffin took the time to explain the law and how even the Almighty California Board of Nursing was not above the Law. Griffin’s meticulous attention to detail, legal acumen, his tenacity, and eventual courtroom presence resulted in his exposing the Board’s injustice: On June 24, 2025, in The Superior Court of the State of California, the Honorable James C. Chalfant granted my 2024 Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandamus; and a Judgment and Writ were issued remanding the Board not only to reverse revocation of my nursing license but its “decision in its entirety.” In the end, the Honorable Chalfant stated “I don’t know why the Board did what it did” in reference to the Board’s refusal to reverse the revocation.
Griffin was able to win not only one but two extremely challenging cases in which the odds were against us. His ability to navigate two very difficult legal cases was exceptional, for which I am indebted; but I am in awe that he was available to answer questions and keep me informed about every step of the process when he had so much work to do for me and other clients. He provided reassurance, alleviated anxiety, gave me hope during both cases, and genuinely cared about my situation and me as a person.
Months later, I was finally able to have my trial and was found innocent by the Jury of the DUI of Drug charge. I received a call from Griffin, who congratulated me with such joy! I was stunned that he knew nearly every major detail of the case, and it turned out that he had been following along every day of the trial. Even after he stopped being my lawyer, he still cared.
Outstanding insights and excellent legal writing helped us prepare and file an excellent brief. I appreciated their responsiveness, support and collaboration.

Griffin Schindler and the team at Decker Law are exceptional. I engaged Decker Law on a complex appellate issue and Griffin and the team came through with flying colors. I make the highest recommendation possible for Decker law, both as a long-time licensed attorney in California and as a client.

Thank you, Decker Law!

I have worked with both James and Griffin and they are always on your side!  They take the time to explain your options and keep on top of your case so they are always looking out for you.  I would wholeheartedly recommend them for any appeals issues you may have.

James Decker is an exceptional attorney for the following reasons:  James, is very competent, works for you, very honest, and very sympathetic to your financial situation in terms of fees.  He will be very honest about the cost and the best route to win your case.  He presents all options and respects how you would like to proceed legally. He never pressures you and informs you through the entire legal process and always meets deadlines.
James, is the most efficient and honest attorney I have come across in my NINE years dealing with family law and the court system.
James Decker is honest, fair and stays within your budget as appeals can be costly.  You will not regret having him represent you.  He knows law and cares!

I had heard great things about Decker Law and their reputation for providing top-notch legal
representation, so l decided to reach out to them for assistance with a fraudulent real estate transaction that l had inadvertently become involved in. Despite being prepared to accept full responsibility for my error, I was disappointed by the lack of communication from Mr. Decker and his team.
After sending several important filings and documents to the firm, I never received a phone call back or any form of acknowledgment. This lack of follow-up was extremely frustrating, especially considering Decker Law's esteemed five-star rating.
As a potential client seeking competent legal representation, I expected a higher level of customer service and responsiveness from a firm of their caliber.
In the legal field, timely communication and attentiveness to client needs are crucial, and unfortunately, my experience with Decker Law left much to be desired in these areas. While I cannot speak to their legal expertise, I believe that maintaining strong communication and providing quality customer service should be paramount for any reputable law firm.

Blue Orbs as Ghosts/Spirits
Some people theorize that a blue orb is a ghost. The blue color radiates from calm and benevolent spirits. This type of encounter is considered as someone the person who is seeing the orb knew, such as a caring and loving parent. However, it can also be that of a pure spirit, such as a child.
Intensity of Color
Some people identify messages based on the intensity of the blue color. For example:
•    A light blue orb conveys a message and/or emotion of peace, calmness and even tranquility.
•    A medium blue orb may be present as a sign of spiritual protection, leading to a feeling of reassurance and safety.
•    A dark blue orb is less common. It is sometimes interpreted as the orb of a spirit that survived turmoil and hardships through purity of spirit.

This is Uncle C’s Spirit!!

I urge anyone who may experience workplace issues to contact the Calderone Law Firm for an initial consultation.

Thank you Vincent & Caroline for your excellent work.

Sincerely,

Vacationing in Cabo

It's been a few months since my approval. As much as I wanted to shout it from the rooftops and leave a review immediately, I chose to sit with the experience and truly soak it all in first.
The process is, of course, overwhelming and in my case, long which is exactly why having the right team matters. From day one, Attorney Patrick was sincere, confident, and consistently encouraging, making me feel reassured that my case was in capable hands. As I reached the final stages, Attorney Ivan and Lexy were incredibly professional and endlessly patient with my many questions, never once making me feel rushed or dismissed.
Despite the political climate, my interview went exceptionally well, so well, in fact, that the officer praised Attorney Ivan during the interview itself. That moment alone spoke volumes about the level of preparation and advocacy this office provides.
I am forever grateful for this team and this office. Their dedication, professionalism, and genuine care made all the difference.
(My first trip was just south of the border, still emotional and meaningful)

reviews.text: I had been hesitant to post this but it’s been 10 MONTHS since “you are on the waitlist for a call back, we will reach out”. At first things were going okay, but our meetings were rushed at times and while we were in the office with the attorney he would have other clients on zoom (mics off) but why couldn’t he give all of us priority in terms of time management with each appointment? Why hold 2 appointments at the same time? Needless to say we called back multiple times and we kept getting the same response “waitlist”. It’s understandable they are busy but 10 months and still nothing? Our case wasn’t pro bono/free, we were paying our dues and doing our due diligence checking in for about 2 months afterwards. We are at a better place now. I wanted to share this because I found it unfair and unkind.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Ms. Basurto,

First and foremost, I am incredibly sorry to hear about your experience. I can promise you that this is not the level of service we strive to provide our clients. De verdad, we take these matters very seriously.

I took the time to double-check our records to see if I could find a match for your name to address any communication issues personally, but I haven't been able to locate your file in our system yet.

Rodrigo

In the beginning their legal team seemed to be knowledgeable and  responsive,. But , we later found out that they were not well versed in basic construction law or current employment laws.  After having the new contract, we began researching if our contract actually  protected the company as well as our customers. We were concerned about our new contract and the content. Soon after,  we put a corporate attorney on retainer. Seeing as they specialize in construction law, they found our  contract did NOT adhere to the Ca. State home improvement contract laws.  Over all the company provides a good service for the minutes and for us that is where  it ends. As contractors in California, we need our contracts to be detailed and accurate as possible in order to be compliant in the states eyes.  We needed a law firm that specializes in our field, which we now have.
They are professional

reviews.text:
Horrible Experience with United Employees Law Group

I had an extremely disappointing and frustrating experience with United Employees Law Group. They referred my case to an incompetent law firm that did absolutely nothing but waste my time. Despite trusting them to handle my case, they allowed the statute of limitations to run out, rendering my case useless. To make matters worse, once they realized their negligence, they had the audacity to drop my case entirely!

Their lack of professionalism, communication, and care for their clients is shocking. I feel completely betrayed by their incompetence, and I strongly advise anyone considering using United Employees Law Group to stay away. They do not have your best interests in mind, and they clearly cannot handle cases properly. Avoid them at all costs!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Elon,

Thank you for sharing your thoughts, however, I was surprised to receive this review, as my firm did not accept your case or represent you.

While we did our best to guide you with resources for finding alternative legal assistance, we understand that your experiences with another firm may have impacted your perspective.

My professional reputation is of utmost importance to me, and we truly do our best to serve every employee that contacts our firm to the best of our abilities. I extend our apologies if you feel that the resources we provided were not adequate, however, I kindly ask you to reconsider this review for the foregoing reasons.

Warm regards,

Walter Haines
Attorney at Law

I had the privilege of being represented
by Brock Gonzales LLP for a discrimination case, and I can confidently say that my experience with this firm exceeded every expectation. From the very beginning, the entire team demonstrated the highest level of professionalism, efficiency, and care.

My attorney, Christopher, guided me through every step of the process with patience, transparency, and genuine concern for my well-being. He made sure I understood each stage of my case and always took the time to answer my questions thoroughly. What stood out most was how the firm not only promised results—but delivered on them.

The team worked diligently to ensure I received the best possible settlement. Their expertise, dedication, and compassion made a stressful situation much easier to handle. I truly felt supported and valued as a client.

I highly recommend Broklen Gonzales LLP to anyone seeking strong legal representation, especially in cases involving employment discrimination. They are a team of true professionals who fight for justice with integrity and commitment.

reviews.text:
Brock called and asked the same questions as intake just to say he won’t take my case, had he not read the intake notes why waste my time. my ex-employer created a falsified performance improvement plan as a strategy to terminate me. This was highly discouraging for Mr. Brock. He just can’t fathom the idea that an employer can actually strategize and create a falsified performance improvement plan. Yes, it happens .
A strategy that out smarts Brock but not all law firms agree with Brocks who appears to be searching for the easy flip cases with no challenge. As soon as I brought up marital status discrimination Brock quickly said I made too many complaints. There is no limit to protected activity and complaints of the employer is guilty of the fact. Brock is a flaw in the system the type of attorney that empowers employers to engage in unlawful activity, but not all attorneys agree I did find Attorney who understands my case. I was able to write a demand letter. If you experience the same denial from Brock, don’t be discouraged in finding a better attorney Brock that voice of the devil’s advocate, keep searching don’t let Brock discourage you from the justice you deserve.

reviews.text:
Aaron Brock is the epitome of professionalism. Without him, I could not have navigated the tumultuous waters to safety the way my case was resolved. I found myself in an unfortunate and unexpected situation at work, just after Thanksgiving. Not an ideal time, to say the least. After speaking with many friends and colleagues at work, I realized I was even more of a victim than I had realized. Aaron and his staff came to my aid and took on my case, despite it being the end of the year and his firm already having a heavy caseload.

Throughout the whole process, I was impressed by Aaron's patience and strategy. There were several times Aaron made moves I would have never thought of, and he always explained his tactics and rationale very clearly when I asked. In the end, Aaron's expertise paid off and I was very happy with the resolution to my case. I don't think there could have been a better outcome; even several of my lawyer friends were impressed. I highly recommend Mr. Brock and his firm to anyone experiencing work-related problems.

In July 2022 I filed a lawsuit against the company I worked at for 33 years. The original attorney who agreed to represent me failed to give me a proper representation. Through no fault of my case in 2024 this attorney's license was revoked due to a felony charge with a trial scheduled. The opposing Council filed the summary judgment in April 2024 to dismiss my case without informing me. Aaron Brock agreed to take my case. When I reached out to his Law Firm, he displayed strong ethics and kindness, and told me that most attorneys do not treat their clients in this manner. I knew at that moment Aaron was the right person. He petitioned the court to continue my case. Through Aaron's advocacy a settlement was agreed upon in 2025. I would like to convey to anyone who reads my testimony that Aaron’s expertise as an attorney is coupled with his humanity, ethics and kindness.

Sincerely,
Marianne L.

I started the SSI application process a while back for my special needs daughter. I found the offices of Tony Adderley while doing a Google search and decided to give them a call because of the positive reviews (yes, I am the type of person who checks for high scores/feedback). From the very first call I was treated with the outmost respect and excellent professionalism from Debbie Canillo. She was very patient with me as I explained my desire to apply on behalf of my daughter for SSI and she explained in detail what the process would be like during the intake.

A day or two later, the case manager for my daughter called, just like Debbie said they would and she was just as patient, friendly and professional. My daughter’s case manager Marie has been on top of our case answering all call’s, questions and concerns I have had about Social Security Administration or keeping me updated on any new developments.

We just had an appointment with an SSA evaluator and hopefully our claim will be approved soon. I have been so happy and satisfied with Debbie and Marie’s work and professionalism that I recommend them to my nephew. He had signed with MORGAN & MORGAN to be his representatives for more than a year and a half and they didn’t do anything for his case. In a period of a year, he had like 6 different people assigned to his case and they never returned his calls or emails. It got so bad that his claim was denied twice and I was the one filling out the paperwork because the people at MORGAN & MORGAN were nowhere to be found.

I most definitely recommend The law offices of Tony Adderley to represent anyone going through the hard and complex process of applying for SSI/SSDI. I haven’t met Mr. Adderley personally yet but Debbie and Marie are exemplary representatives of his office and the courteous and professional services you will receive from this firm.

Declined My Retaliation Case After Months of Delay — While the Clock Ran Out.

Barrera & Associates had my case details as early as March. It involved whistleblower retaliation, wrongful termination, and Medi-Cal fraud at a psychiatric facility. I provided them with documentation, emails, HR records—everything they asked for. Their paralegal even followed up to confirm my interest and requested additional materials. I complied every time.

Then, after weeks of silence, they finally declined to represent me in June. Not because the case lacked merit—but because of “workload.” By then, months had passed and my statutory deadlines were dangerously close.

Let’s be honest: this isn’t about bandwidth. It’s about fear. No one wants to touch a case that exposes institutional fraud tied to state funds. So instead of being transparent from the jump, they slow-walked it and left me hanging.

If you’re facing real retaliation—don’t count on this firm. They may say they fight for workers, but when the fight gets ugly, they disappear.

I think this kind of professionalism and quality of attorney is what every lawyer should aspire. I am very pleased with the outcome. Thank you Brian Poulter and Company for everything.

So here is the skinny on Brian, I came across him by completely random luck.  And let me tell you, it couldn't have worked out better.  I was injured when visiting CA and about 6 weeks after my accident I started reaching out to attorneys.  After contacting about 8-10 law firms large and small, I couldn't get a call back from anyone to discuss my case.  My thoughts were there wasn't enough money to be had for the large law firms to put in the effort.  My injuries healed and were not life threating.  But still I wanted to talk to someone to see if I had a case.  Finally, I reached out to a law firm in my home state.  Well someone there just happened to attend law school with a personal injury attorney in CA.  So I contacted that attorney, and that attorney wasn't able to help me due to the facts of my particular case, but that attorney put me in contact with Brian.  Like I said random dumb luck.  Brian agreed to represent me, and I couldn't be happier.  After the initial consult I sat back and let Brian do his thing.  He would check in from time to time with me.  Staff would send forms for me to fill out as deadlines came due.  I never had any issues getting in contact with him or his office.  He was gracious enough to even give his cell number, so should I ever have any questions, I could have reached out to him.  Now I didn't have a lot of questions, I hired Brian to let Brian do the work.  If you are going to hire an attorney, you have to trust that the attorney is going to always put your interests first.  I never doubted it.  He would give his advice, and then let you make the decision.  He was great.  His staff was great.  If you are considering an attorney, in my opinion, you can't go wrong by selecting him to represent you.  You won't be disappointed!
Star was great. She explained everything and we really appreciated her.

They stole my money and never delivered the filing that I paid for. Do not pay by Zelle or cash by getting lured by the discount.  When I escalated the issue, they said that the employee who charged me no longer worked there and this was not their problem because she was the one who scammed me. I was the customer of the business, not the customer of the employee! The owner, who is supposedly an attorney,  obviously does not know the basic underlying law that the employees of a company are the representatives of that company. Otherwise, no one would ever be able to sue businesses for frauding and scramming the customers on business premises and with business services but, instead, would have to sue individual employees. Thankfully, I was able to do a charge back on my credit card. If this business has scammed you, please report them to the Los Angeles County Department of Consumer and Business Affairs.

reviews.text:
To CHP

It unfortunate that you continue to lie about the actual events. You did not resolve the problem. In fact, Sandra totally mishandle my case. As mentioned previously,  on the day for the case to be dismissed, there was no court hearing schedule, and it was not on the court calendar because Sandra nor anyone from your office was present at court. therefore, I do not understand what you mean about arriving 40 minutes late. If this office claims they have experience and deal with these types of cases how would they know the case was dismissed when Sandra, nor anyone one  from your office was present in court that day. You are referring to the first court appearance when the office manager/ called me to tell me Sandra was at the house when she clearly was not arriving for over 40 minutes.  Second, you stated you made a deal and you actually did not when clearly I had the paper work to dismiss case from city which you ignored and did not do. I would have never signed that document I should and that is on me. You can sit there and make yourselves look like you helped me and won my case but you actually did not. All you did is destroy my record. But keep telling yourselves that if this is what is going to make you feel better about yourselves, saying you helped me when you completely fouled up my case and robbed me. Please do not embarrass yourselves any further before as I have obtained the document as proof that this case has not been dismissed and awaiting for court hearing. I am not the only one who you guys done wrong as I seen past clients reviews who also agree that you guys are complete garbage.

Eviction matter,  had letter from city of landlord non compliance therefore the three day notice or quit notice is  nulled and void. I even paid for Ms Sandra who was helping in the case to come speak on my behalf however when I wanted to  cancel  her services because I had the letter from coty was told no refund but because I had no heard from her since to prepare for court date. Day of court  she showed up late was told she was there when she was not. When she did show up she went behind my back to talk to plaintiff's laywer. She told me that she reached an agreement that case will be dismissed and sealed if I left by certain date and will receive  part of my deposit back. She further stated to return back to court even judge  at  certain date and time which was noted in court documents and case will be dismissed if I fulfiled te agreement . On day of court , case was not on calender was only sealed and have this judgement on record. When I set up an appointment to speak with Ms Sandra I was met by coworker/ office manager and  he stated Sandra did her job that there was nothing they could do.If I was to see the judge was only to if Idid not agree with terms. Sandra guaranteed me that case will be dismissed because letter from city  they would have to start eviction process all over now but lcsme to agreement to eave to leave on specific date and part of deposit to be return to me and casexwill be dimmed or I would never had signed document.  Her office manager/co worker  also stated   maybe got dismissed which made no sense because there was no court hearing for it to dismiss case but told to call court to see if it shows up in the minutes. I was lied too and cheated by these people. They took advantage of me in the worst way when I was at the most vulnerable time in my life. They knew they did wrong because they would not let me speak or see Ms Sandra nor dishes follow up with me regarding out come  nor genuine interest in to help me when I clearly there was evidence landlord was in the wrong to evicted us and now a judgement sealng of this case was not the agreement. Sandra and her office just wiped their hands clean and state no wrong doing.Fortunately I was able to get thru the judicial secretary at courthouse to have case heard and added  calenderfor future date and get rhis case  have this dismissed and removed from my record as documented on court agreement. Lesson learned  my advice do not trust these people  when it comes to legal matters consult actual lawyers  Unfortunately due to money and time this was last resort.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for reaching out with your feedback. First and foremost, we celebrate with you on the dismissal of your case and the sealing of your record, a testament to the diligence and precision in our preparation of your legal documents. The intricacies of the legal journey often present complexities; our team is dedicated to demystifying these processes for you, striving for clarity and simplicity amidst the inherently detailed nature of legal and court proceedings.

Understanding that this landscape can sometimes breed frustration or confusion—often stemming from factors beyond our control—we prioritize open, clear communication. Our goal is to ensure you feel empowered to seek clarification on any aspect of your case, to which we respond with straightforward and thoughtful explanations.

It is clear that in your situation, despite our efforts to align on expectations and processes, there was a divergence in understanding our role and the procedures involved. Notably, on the day of your appointment—arriving 40 minutes behind schedule—we chose to proceed with our discussion rather than postpone, recognizing the importance of moving forward with the explanation of the case dismissal process, despite having other clients scheduled.

Your case's resolution, involving the dismissal and sealing of your eviction record, aligned perfectly with your initial objectives. This outcome was achieved through careful negotiation and agreement with the Plaintiff, culminating in the submission of all necessary paperwork and the finalization of the stipulated judgment.

We truly appreciate the opportunity to have worked towards and successfully reached your desired outcome—avoiding an eviction on your record. As we move forward, we wish you nothing but the best in all your future endeavors.

Sincerely,
CPH
Thank you for your patronage.

He has handled a number of situations for my family, and he consistently handled each one the same way - EXCEPTIONALLY!  Mr. Goldstein is very generous with his time and gives wonderful advice because he really cares about you.  We have prevailed against the toughest of adversaries and have won each and every time.

I recently had a situation that was very traumatic for my family to go through, and Mr. Goldstein & his team really went the extra mile and made us feel that they had our back and really understood what we were going through, physically and emotionally.  We had peace of mind and 100% confidence that we were in the right hands.  Great communication, a great proactive approach to righting a wrong that had happened to us, extraordinary in navigating the legal system, quick responses to all of our questions, adapted to unpredictable situations like a best friend would, and finalized a settlement to get us compensation as fast as possible.  Final result was life changing and far above anything we could have expected.

THANK YOU!
No nonsense No BS

I was seriously injured in a car wreck over Christmas when a loved one called Mike. He was very kind and compassionate while advising me. Mike explained the process and was always polite and direct, as was his staff.
The legal system moves at a glacial pace, but Mike kept things moving. Also, he’s not the type to knuckle under with a bad offer. I was never uncomfortable nor was I talked down to. Mike is super chill and respected my goals as a client.
He is an intelligent, highly competent, and motivated attorney. Mike is thorough and willing to exhaust all options. He not only met my expectations, he exceeded them. I would highly recommend him to everyone.

To all interested,

I have first meet David Shraga of KAWAHITO SHRAGA & WESTRICK , three years ago when David represented me in arbitration against former client. He has don such fantastic job that i have engaged his services again when much more complicated case was brought up against me and my company. Again the results David and his firm achieved were above my expectations.

If I have to go thru the legal process again I will feel very comfortable knowing that Mr David Shraga, Mr Kawahito and Mr Westrick are there with me.

Great law firm to partner up with. Their team of lawyers are keen to understand your business and processes so you get applicable advice. In additon, they are extremely knowledgable in corporate and employment law and are always accesible regardless of the nature of your legal issue.
James Kawahito worked intelligently and quickly for my legal needs.

Shawn Westrick along with his team has handled several complex legal situations for my firm and everything came out in our favor.  Professional group, extremely knowledgeable, and great and friendly approach to dealing with clients.

I called and spoke with Eddie regarding a loan I requested through my attorney. I agreed to a loan for $5k back in September 2024. The case was settled in October 2025 so the loan should have been paid with back with interest. According to Eddie, he said my account reflected that I took a loan out in January 2021 for $9,100 which accrued interest of $24k. I never took out that loan in 2021. When I asked Eddie how much was paid back by attorney he told me that it’s just showing “settled” and I have to call back on Monday. I had to ask him about the $5k that I borrowed in 2024 and he said “oh, I see that one too.” Between my attorney and Rockpoint I believe there’s something unscrupulous going on. Supposedly, $34k was deducted from my settlement to pay back Rockpoint. Did Rockpoint get that or did my legal counsel? Yes, the customer service was great while trying to obtain the loan but the end results were disappointing at best.

Rockpoint is the best they gave me 15k
And then I asked for another 10k and it was no problem in times of need rockpoint comes through for you.....

reviews.text: I was dealing with a troubling legal matter and was recommended through a friend that Ed was a very good lawyer.  Even though my situation did not directly involve his specialty areas, he still took the time to listen to me and then made calls on my behalf.  He was able to connect with me to another lawyer who eventually was able to work with me through my legal matter.  What I’m grateful to Ed is that he put my needs before his own and diid not give up on me.  It says a lot about who he is.  Ed could have simply said, “I’m sorry, I can’t help you” and let me continue my search on my own but he didn’t. Dear Debbie, we’re so proud to help our clients better understand the value and peace of mind that comes with having an estate plan in place.
